questions.statement:
<p><strong>Choose appropriate articles for the given sentence:</strong></p>

<p>My uncle lives in ___home for ___elderly.</p>
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is '2' i.e. a, the .

Key Points

  • The sentence is discussing a specific type of place ("home for the elderly"), which requires the article "the" to indicate specificity.
  • The article "a" is used with "home" to introduce the concept of a general place without specifying which home.
  • Correct article usage ensures clarity and grammatical accuracy in English sentences.

Correct sentence: My uncle lives in a home for the elderly.

Additional Information

  • Usage of Articles:
    • "A/An": Used to refer to non-specific or general items. Example: "I saw a dog."
    • "The": Used to refer to specific or known items. Example: "I saw the dog near the park."
  • Situational Context:
    • In this sentence, "home" is being introduced as a general concept, so "a" is used.
    • "Elderly" refers to a specific group, which makes "the" appropriate.

questions.statement:
<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">Directions: Select the segment of the sentence that contains a grammatical error. If there is no error mark &#39;No error&#39; as your answer.</span></p>

<p>All one&#39;s endless longing for/ (A) life&#39;s pleasures is&nbsp;stilled on/ (B) the shores of an Arabian Sea./ (C) No error/ (D)</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">Identify the part of the sentence that contains an error.</span></p>

<p>Whatever she found at the Peak had driven her into the&nbsp;forest&nbsp;full of insurgents despite her injury and her lack of familiarity with the&nbsp;forest&nbsp;or the world outside hers.</p>
options: despite her injury and her lack of familiarity /
Whatever she found at the Peak had /
with the&nbsp;forest&nbsp;or the world outside hers. /
driven her into the&nbsp;forest&nbsp;full of insurgents /

questions.statement:
<p><span style="font-weight: 700;" wfd-id="4365">In the following question, one part of the sentence may have an error. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and click the option corresponding to it. If the sentence is free from error, click option (4) i.e No error</span></p>

<div wfd-id="4364">(a) Who do you think (b) Time will feature (c) person of the year for 2020? (d) No error</div>

<p><strong>Choose appropriate articles for the given sentence:</strong></p>

<p>____ new carpet was laid out in ____ drawing-room today.</p>

<p><strong>Choose appropriate article for the given sentence:</strong></p>

<p>I studied _____French in high school for four years.</p>

<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700; white-space: pre-wrap;">Select the segment of the sentence that contains the grammatical error. If there is no error, mark &#39;No error&#39; as your answer.</span></p>

<div style="">Milk cake of Alwar A)/ is known for its superior B)/ quality and authenticity. C)/ No error D)</div>

<p><strong>Fill in the blanks with grammatically correct option. </strong></p>

<p>When Lulu asked to see _____ wedding pictures, she didn&rsquo;t expect Annie to put on _____ twelve-hour slide show.&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>Choose appropriate articles for the given sentence:</strong></p>

<p>____super moon occurs when ____moon&rsquo;s orbit is at its closest to Earth and at ____same time it is full.</p>

<p><strong>Improve the bracketed part of the sentence.</strong></p>

<p>Kiran will bring (small gift to Chandu&rsquo;s party).</p>
options: the small gift to Chandu&rsquo;s party /
an small gift to Chandu&rsquo;s party /
a small gift to Chandu&rsquo;s party /
